spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates the exact reason overallProgress exists per the spec — identical 0..1 reading for both a document-timeline animation and a ScrollTimeline, with side-by-side comparison.

interactive.rationale: Time animation runs autonomously; scroll animation is driven by scrolling the inner scroller, with live currentTime + overallProgress readouts and progress bars for both.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises 'one number, two timelines' running side by side; demo delivers exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Handles the ScrollTimeline missing case ('not supported here') but doesn't cover paused/cancelled animations, scrolltimeline with axis: 'x', or what happens when the scroller is unscrollable (content shorter than viewport).

references.rationale: Links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5083257285378048 and the drafts.csswg.org Web Animations 2 spec anchor.

design_system.rationale: Variables used throughout; the unrelated boilerplate live-probe panel was removed on 2026-06-04.

openQuestions.title: Re-review after generic probe removal
openQuestions.detail: The unrelated generated live-probe controls were removed on 2026-06-04. Use chrome-devtools-mcp to re-test the remaining concept UI; if the page is now static or lacks a feature-specific observable behavior, add a real control, readout, server-backed route, or exact feature probe rather than restoring the generic panel.

openQuestions.title: Cover ScrollTimeline axis: 'x' or view-progress
openQuestions.detail: The whole point of the comparison is timeline independence — adding a third panel with a ViewTimeline (or axis: 'x' ScrollTimeline) would reinforce that overallProgress works uniformly across all timeline kinds, not just two.

openQuestions.title: Show the paused / finished state
openQuestions.detail: Both animations run continuously — there's no Pause control or Reset, so a reader can't compare what overallProgress reports when the underlying playState changes. Add play/pause for both panels.

summary: Best of the five concepts for showing the spec's headline benefit — timeline-independence — and it does so with a real ScrollTimeline alongside a time animation. Drop the boilerplate live-probe card and add a paused state for full coverage. 2026-06-04 maintenance removed the unrelated generic live-probe panel; this critique needs chrome-devtools-mcp re-review before treating the interactivity score as current.
